questions.question: 中文导读：CLIP's contrastive loss is symmetric (image-to-text + text-to-image). Why both directions?
options: 中文选项：Numerical stability
中文选项：You want both queries to work at inference: text-to-image retrieval and image-to-text retrieval. Training only one direction makes the other direction's accuracy drop significantly
中文选项：Because loss must sum to zero
中文选项：Symmetry is required by PyTorch
questions.explanation: 中文解析：The embedding space needs to be symmetric across modalities because downstream tasks query in both directions. Zero-shot classification is text-to-image (classify image given text prompts). Retrieval can go either way. Training with only i2t or only t2i leaves the other direction weak.

questions.question: 中文导读：Zero-shot classification with CLIP works by... ?
options: 中文选项：Running a classifier head trained on ImageNet
中文选项：Encoding prompts like 'a photo of a dog' for each candidate class, encoding the test image, and taking argmax of cosine similarities between the image embedding and all class text embeddings
中文选项：Fine-tuning the image encoder on the class
中文选项：Searching a database of labelled examples
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Zero-shot means no task-specific training: the model's representations already let you compare images to arbitrary text descriptions. Write one prompt per class, encode all prompts and the test image, cosine similarity, argmax. The only 'trick' is prompt engineering — using multiple templates per class and averaging their embeddings gains 1-3 top-1 on ImageNet.

questions.question: 中文导读：SigLIP replaces CLIP's softmax with a sigmoid loss per pair. What benefit does that give?
options: 中文选项：Faster GPU kernels
中文选项：The sigmoid loss is per-pair, so it does not depend on the batch as a normalisation denominator. SigLIP trains well at smaller batch sizes where CLIP's softmax loss starves for negatives
中文选项：Lower memory use
中文选项：Better accuracy on COCO only
questions.explanation: 中文解析：CLIP's symmetric cross-entropy is a softmax over the whole batch, so effective negatives = batch_size - 1. Small batches starve it. SigLIP is per-pair: each (image, caption) pair gets a binary decision (match or not). No batch-level normalisation, so SigLIP works at batch 128 while CLIP needs 8192. At equal scale SigLIP matches or beats CLIP.

questions.question: 中文导读：A practitioner reports 88% zero-shot top-1 on CIFAR-10 with CLIP ViT-B/32 and 90% with the same model using 80 prompt templates per class. Why does template averaging help?
options: 中文选项：It doubles the dataset

中文选项：Different templates activate different aspects of the text encoder's learned distribution; averaging smooths the class embedding over the manifold of plausible natural-language descriptions of the class, producing a more robust centroid
中文选项：It reduces variance in logit_scale
中文选项：80 is the magic number for CLIP
questions.explanation: 中文解析：Each template is a different natural-language cue. 'a photo of a dog' emphasises one aspect; 'a blurry photo of a dog' another; 'a sketch of a dog' yet another. Averaging the text embeddings gives a smoother representation of the concept 'dog' that is less sensitive to individual-prompt quirks. The OpenAI CLIP paper published 80 templates that lift ImageNet zero-shot by ~2 points.

questions.question: 中文导读：Why do modern VLMs (LLaVA, Qwen-VL, InternVL) use a CLIP-family vision encoder instead of a supervised ImageNet ResNet?
options: 中文选项：CLIP encoders are faster

中文选项：CLIP features are aligned with natural language, so the LLM can reason about them with less adaptation; supervised ImageNet features were never trained against captions and need heavy projection layers to bridge to text
中文选项：ResNets cannot see colour
中文选项：It is a licensing requirement
questions.explanation: 中文解析：A VLM bolts a vision encoder to a language model and trains a small projection. CLIP-style encoders were trained against text, so their outputs already live in a space the LLM can consume with a few linear layers of adaptation. Supervised ImageNet encoders have no notion of natural-language structure and require much larger bridging MLPs to work with an LLM. This is why every SOTA VLM uses a CLIP-family vision tower.
